title: Robot Car
subtitle: ESP32 based smart robotic car with L298N motor control
desc: Build a wireless robot car using ESP32, motor driver and DC motors!
fullDesc: A smart robotic car project using ESP32 microcontroller, L298N motor driver and four DC motors. The robot can move in different directions by controlling motor speed and direction.
author: Atul Sawant
innovation: A low-cost educational robotic platform designed to teach students embedded systems, motor control and robotics concepts.
problemStatement: Students need practical exposure to robotics hardware integration and real-time control systems.
solutionApproach: Used ESP32 as the main controller with L298N motor driver to operate four DC motors. The system provides a flexible platform for robotics experiments and future IoT integration.

hardwareSpecs.powerConsumption: Depends on motor load and battery capacity
hardwareSpecs.communicationProtocol: GPIO based motor control, WiFi/Bluetooth supported by ESP32

guide: Connect ESP32 GPIO pins with L298N motor driver input pins
Connect four DC motors to L298N output terminals
Connect battery supply to motor driver power input
Provide regulated power supply to ESP32
Upload motor control program using Arduino IDE
Test forward, backward, left and right movement
Optimize motor speed using PWM control

codeFiles.name: robot_car_control.ino
codeFiles.desc: ESP32 motor control program for robot car movement

youtubeVideos.title: Robot Car Demo
youtubeVideos.desc: Demonstration of ESP32 based robot car movement
